title: 第 99 節
en: 1 It sprang from water and the other elements. Comm. Professor Weber proposes to translate apa/$ by Erlangungen, acquisitions, with reference to apnoshi, 'how dost thou acquire my names ? ' in what precedes.
2 Who knows the conditioned and mythological form of Brahman as here described, sitting on the couch.
Second Adhyaya.

i. Prawa (breath)1 is Brahman, thus says Kaushitaki. Of this pra7za, which is Brahman, the mind (manas) is the messenger, speech the housekeeper, the eye the guard, the ear the informant. He who knows mind as the messenger of pra;?a, which is Brahman, becomes possessed of the messenger. He who knows speech as the housekeeper, becomes possessed of the housekeeper. He who knows the eye as the guard, becomes possessed of the guard. He who knows the ear as the informant, becomes possessed of the informant.

Now to that pra?za, which is Brahman, all these deities (mind, speech, eye, ear) bring an offering, though he asks not for it, and thus to him who knows this all creatures brinor an offering, thou eh he asks not for it. For him who knows this, there is this Upanishad (secret vow), ' Beg not !' As a man who has begged through a village and got nothing sits down and says, ' I shall never eat anything given by those people,' and as then those who formerly refused him press him (to accept their alms), thus is the rule for him who begs not, but the charitable will press him and say, * Let us give to thee.'

2. Pra;*a (breath) is Brahman, thus says Paingya. And in " that pra;m, which is Brahman, the eye 1 In the first chapter it vas said, 'He approaches the couch Amitau^as, that is pra«a, breath, spirit, life. Therefore having explained in the first adhyaya the knowledge of the couch (of Brahman), the next subject to be explained is the knowledge of prawa, the living spirit, taken for a time as Brahman, or the last cause of everything.'

II ADHYAYA, 7. 28 1 stands firm behind speech, the ear stands firm behind the eye, the mind stands firm behind the ear, and the spirit stands firm behind the mind \ To that pra;2a, which is Brahman, all these deities bring an offering, though he asks not for it, and thus to him who knows this, all creatures bring an offering, though he asks not for it. For him who knows this, there is this Upanishad (secret vow), 'Beg not!' As a man who has begged through a village and got nothing sits down and says, ' I shall never eat anything given by those people,' and as then those who formerly refused him press him (to accept their alms), thus is the rule for him who begs not, but the charitable will press him and say, ' Let us give to thee.'

3. Now follows the attainment of the highest treasure (scil. prawa, spirit2). If a man meditates on that highest treasure, let him on a full moon or a new moon, or in the bright fortnight, under an auspicious Nakshatra, at one of these proper times, bending his right knee, offer oblations of ghee with a ladle (sruva), after having placed the fire, swept the ground 3, strewn the sacred grass, and sprinkled water. Let him say : ' The deity called Speech is 1 I translate vakparastat, ^akshu^parastat, mana^parastat as compounds, and read jrotraparastat. The commentator requires this. He says that speech is uncertain, and has to be checked by the eye. The eye is uncertain, taking mother of pearl for silver, and must be checked by the ear. The ear is uncertain, and must be checked by the mind, for unless the mind is attentive, the ear hears not. The mind, lastly, depends on the spirit, for without spirit there is no mind. The commentator is right in reading rundhe or runddhe instead of rundhate.
2 The vital spirits are called the highest treasure, because a man surrenders everything to preserve his vital spirits or his life.
3 Cf. Brih. At. VI, 3, 1.
the attainer, may it attain this for me from him (who possesses and can bestow what I wish for). Svaha. to it!'
' The deity called pra^a (breath) is the attainer, may it attain this for me from him. Svaha to it ! '
' The deity called the eye is the attainer, may it attain this for me from him. Svaha to it ! '
' The deity called the ear is the attainer, may it attain this for me from him. Svaha to it ! '
' The deity called mind (manas) is the attainer of it, may it attain this for me from him. Svaha to it'
' The deity called pra^fia (knowledge) is the attainer of it, may it attain this for me from him. Svaha to it!'

Then having inhaled the smell of the smoke, and having rubbed his limbs with the ointment of ghee, walking on in silence, let him declare his wish, or let him send a messenger. He will surely obtain his wish.

4. Now follows the Daiva Smara, the desire to be accomplished by the gods. If a man desires to become dear x to any man or woman, or to any men or women, then at one of the (fore-mentioned) proper times he offers, in exactly the same manner (as before), oblations of ghee, saying : ' I offer thy speech in myself, I (this one here 2), Svaha/ ' I offer thy ear in myself, I (this one here), Svaha.' ' I offer thy 1 As dear as pra;za or life.

2 The commentator explains these mysterious utterances by: 'I offer, I throw, in the fire, which is lit by the fuel of thy indifference or dislike, in myself, being the object of thy love, speech, the organ of speech, of thee, who art going to love me. This one, i.e. I myself, or my love, may prosper. Svaha, my speech, may grant approval to the oblation of me, the lover.'

II ADHYAYA, 6. 283 mind in myself, I (this one here), Svaha.' ' I offer thy pra^na (knowledge) in myself, I (this one here), Svaha.' Then having inhaled the smell of the smoke, and having rubbed his limbs with the ointment of ghee, walking on in silence, let him try to come in contact or let him stand speaking in the wind, (so that the wind may carry his words to the person by whom he desires to be loved). Surely he becomes dear, and they think of him.

5. Now follows the restraint (sa^yamana) instituted by Pratardana (the son of Divodasa) : they call it the inner Agni-hotra. So long as a man speaks, he cannot breathe, he offers all the while his pra^a (breath) in his speech. And so long as a man breathes, he cannot speak, he offers all the while his speech in his breath. These two endless and immortal oblations he offers always, whether waking or sleeping. Whatever other oblations there are (those, e.g. of the ordinary Agnihotra, consisting of milk and other things), they have an end, for they consist of works (which, like all works, have an end). The ancients, knowing this (the best Agnihotra), did not offer the (ordinary) Agnihotra.

6. Uktha1 is Brahman, thus said -Sushkabhrmgara. Let him meditate on it (the uktha) as the same with the Rik, and all beings will praise him as the best. Let him meditate on it as the same with the Ya^-us, and all beings will join before him 1 Uktha, a Vedic hymn, has been identified with pra;za, breath, in the Kawva and other Sakhas (Brih. Ar. V, 13, 1; Ait. Ar. II, 1, 2). Here uktha, i. e. the pra«a of the uktha, is further identified with Brahman. As uktha (the hymn) is pra;za, and as the sacrifice is performed with hymns, the sacrifice, too, is uktha, and therefore prawa, and therefore Brahman. Comm.

as the best. Let him meditate on it as the same with the Saman, and all beings will bow before him as the best1. Let him meditate on it as the same with might, let him meditate on it as the same with glory, let him meditate on it as the same with splendour. For as the bow is among weapons the mightiest, the most glorious, the most splendid, thus is he who knows this among all beings the mightiest, the most glorious, the most splendid. The Adhvaryu conceives the fire of the altar, which is used for the sacrifice, to be himself. In it he (the Adhvaryu) weaves the Ya^us portion of the sacrifice. And in the Ya^us portion the Hotrz weaves the.Rik portion of the sacrifice. And in the Rik portion the Udgatrz weaves the Saman portion of the sacrifice. He (the Adhvaryu or prana.) is the self of the threefold knowledge ; he indeed is the self of it (of pnma). He who knows this is the self of it (becomes prawa 2).
1 The verbs ark, vug", and sannam are not used idiomatically, but with reference to the words rik, yagus, and saman.

2 The commentator explains this somewhat differently. He takes it to be the object of the last paragraph to show that the Prawa-vidya can ultimately produce final liberation, and not only temporal rewards. The Adhvaryu priest, he says, takes what is called uktha, and has been identified with Rik, Ya^us, and Saman hymns, all contained in the mouth, as being outwardly the sacrificial fire of the altar, because that fire cannot be lighted without such hymns. Thus the self of the Adhvaryu priest becomes identified, not only with the uktha, the hymns, but also with the sacrificial fire, and he meditates on himself as fire, as hymn (uktha), and as breath (pra«a). I read sa esha sarvasyai trayyai vidyaya atma, esha u evasyatma. Etadatma bhavati ya eva?« veda. But if we read asyatma, we cannot with the commentator explain it by asya uktayas trayya atma, but must refer asya to pra«a, breath, life, which is here to be identified with Brahman.

II ADHYAYA, 8. 285 7. Next follow the three kinds of meditation of the all-conquering (sarva^it) Kaushitaki. The allconquering Kaushitaki adores the sun when rising, having put on the sacrificial cord1, having brought water, and having thrice sprinkled the water-cup, saying : ' Thou art the deliverer, deliver me from sin.' In the same manner he adores the sun when in the zenith, saying : ' Thou art the highest deliverer, deliver me highly from sin.' In the same manner he adores the sun when setting, saying :

' Thou art the full deliverer, deliver me fully from sin.' Thus he fully removes whatever sin he committed by day and by night. And in the same manner he who knows this, likewise adores the sun, and fully removes whatever sin he committed by day and by night.

8. Then (secondly) let him worship every month (in the year) at the time of the new moon, the moon as it is seen in the west in the same manner (as before described with regard to the sun), or let him send forth his speech toward the moon with two green blades of grass, saying : ' O thou who art mistress of immortal joy, through that gentle heart of mine . which abides in the moon, may I never weep for misfortune concerning my children.'
The children of him (who thus adores the moon) do not indeed die before him. Thus it is with a man to whom a son is already born.
Now for one to whom no son is born as yet. He mutters the three Rik verses. ' Increase, O Soma ! may vigour come to thee' (Rv. I, 91, 16 ; IX, 31, 4).
zh: 1 它源於水及其他元素。評注：韋伯教授提議以「所得物」（Erlangungen）或「所獲物」來翻譯 apa/$，並參照前文中的 apnoshi（「你如何獲得我的名諱？」）。
誰知曉婆羅門（Brahman）在此所述之有條件與神話形式之人，坐於榻上。
第二章（Adhyaya）
普拉那（breath）即梵天，高希達基如是說。此普拉那既為梵天，心（manas）乃其使者，語言為其家宰，眼睛為其衛士，耳朵為其通報者。凡知心為普拉那之使者、此普拉那即梵天者，彼人遂得有使者。凡知語言為家宰者，彼人遂得有家宰。凡知眼睛為衛士者，彼人遂得有衛士。凡知耳朵為通報者，彼人遂得有通報者。

現在，那個最高的梵（pra?za），即梵天，所有這些提婆（心、言語、眼、耳）都向他獻祭，雖然他並未祈求，因此對於了知此者，所有眾生都向他獻祭，雖然他並未祈求。對於了知此者，這是奧義書（秘密誓願）：「勿乞求！」如同一個人行乞於某村而一無所獲，坐下並說「我絕不接受那些人給予的任何東西」，然後那些曾經拒絕他的人便會催促他（接受他們的施舍），同樣，不乞求者的法則就是：慈善者將會催促他，並說「讓我們給予你。」

2. 生命氣（prāṇa，呼吸）就是梵天，派因迦亞如此說道。而在「那生命氣，它就是梵天」中，眼睛¹ 在第一章中曾說過：「他接近阿彌陀佛斯的臥榻，即生命氣（prāṇa），呼吸、精神、生命。因此在第一個教科書（adhyāya）中已經闡明了對梵天臥榻的知識，接下來要闡述的主題就是對生命氣的知識，即生活精神，暫時被視為梵天或萬物的終極原因。」 --- ¹ 在第一章中曾說過，「他接近阿彌陀佛斯的臥榻，即生命氣（prāṇa），呼吸、精神、生命。因此在第一個教科書（adhyāya）中已經闡明了對梵天臥榻的知識，接下來要闡述的主題就是對生命氣的知識，即生活精神，暫時被視為梵天或萬物的終極原因。」

II ADHYAYA, 7. 28 1 心智立於言語之後，耳立於眼之後，意念立於耳之後，而精神立於意念之後。對於那個是梵天（Brahman）的生命氣（prana），所有這些提婆都帶來供奉，儘管他並未請求，如此對於知道這一點的人，所有生物都帶來供奉，儘管他並未請求。對於知道這一點的人，有這個奧義書（secret vow）：「勿乞求！」如同一個人曾乞求於某個村莊而一無所獲，便坐下來說：「我將永遠不吃那些人所給予的任何東西」，於是曾經拒絕他的人便強烈促請他（接受他們的施捨），如此便是不乞求者的法則，而施捨者將強烈促請他並說：「讓我們給予你。」

3. 次者乃獲得最高之寶藏（即所謂 prawa，精神）。若有人冥想該最高寶藏，則應於圓月或新月之時，或於明亮的月份，在吉祥的納克沙特拉（Nakshatra）之下，於此等適當時刻中，屈其右膝，於置妥火焰、掃淨地面、鋪灑聖草及灑水之後，以杓子（sruva）獻上澄清油脂的祭品。其人應言曰：「名為言語（vakparastat）之提婆乃¹ ¹ 我將 vakparastat、akshuparastat、manaparastat 譯為複合詞，並讀作 jrotraparastat。註釋者對此有所要求。彼謂言語乃不確定者，必須由眼睛加以檢驗。眼睛亦不確定，以珍珠母混淆於白銀，必須由耳朵加以檢驗。耳朵亦不確定，必須由心靈加以檢驗，蓋若心靈不專注，耳朵則無所聞。心靈最後依賴於精神，蓋若無精神，則無心靈。註釋者正確地讀作 rundhe 或 runddhe 而非 rundhate。
生命精氣被稱為最高的寶藏，因為人類會放棄一切以保存其生命精氣或生命。
3 參見《大森林奧義書》(Brih. At.) VI, 3, 1。
祈求者，願他為我獲致此物（他擁有並能賜予我所欲求之物）。Svaha。向它祈求！'
「稱為 pra^a（呼吸）的神祇是獲得者，願它為我從他那裡獲得此物。向它致祀！」
「稱為眼睛的神祇是得者，願它為我從他那裡得到這個。敬禮於它！」（Svaha）
「被稱為耳朵的神祇是成就者，願它為我從他那裡成就此事。向它致敬！」(Svaha)
「被稱為心靈（manas）的神靈是其獲得者，願它為我從他那裡獲得此物。吠陀獻語（Svaha）敬獻於它。」
『稱為般若（knowledge）的神祇乃是其獲得者，願它為我從他處獲得此事。吽！』
然後，在吸入煙霧的氣味，並用酥油膏摩擦四肢之後，沉默地行走，他可以表達他的願望，或派遣一名使者。他必然會實現他的願望。
4. 接著是提婆祈願（Daiva Smara），即欲由諸神而得以成就之事。若有人欲使自己為任何男子或女子，或任何男子或女子所喜愛，則於上述某個適當時刻，他以完全相同的方式獻上酥油祭品，說道：「我在自身中獻上汝之語音，我（此處之人），婆訶（Svaha）！」「我在自身中獻上汝之耳，我（此處之人），婆訶。」「我在自身中獻上汝之……」 1 正如生命原理（prana）或生命一般受歡迎。
評註者對這些神祕的言辭作如下解釋：「我獻祭，我投擲於火中，那火由汝之冷漠或厭惡的燃料所點燃，在我自身之中，作為汝愛戀之對象，言語，言語之器官，屬於汝，汝將要愛我。這一位，即我自身，或我的愛，願得以繁榮。Svaha，我的言語，願許可我之獻祭，即我這戀人的獻祭。」
II 阿地耶亞（ADHYAYA），6. 283 「我在自身中奉獻汝之心意，我（此一者），娑婆訶（Svaha）。」「我在自身中奉獻汝之生氣（pra^na，知識），我（此一者），娑婆訶。」然後，嗅聞煙霧之香氣，以酥油之膏脂塗抹其肢體，無言地行進，讓他試圖接觸，或讓他站立而言語於風中（俾風得以將其言語傳達至彼人，為彼人之所愛慕），必然他變得可愛，而眾人憶念於他。

5. 現在接著說由普剌塔爾達那（Pratardana，第烏瓦達薩之子）所制定的節制（sa^yamana）：人們稱之為內在的阿格尼祭（inner Agni-hotra）。只要人在說話，他就無法呼吸，他始終在言語中奉獻他的生氣（pra^a，呼吸）。同樣地，只要人在呼吸，他就無法說話，他始終在呼吸中奉獻他的言語。這兩種無盡無窮且不朽的奉獻，他始終在奉獻，無論醒著或睡著。凡有其他種種奉獻（例如普通的阿格尼祭，由乳汁及其他物質組成），它們都有終盡，因為它們由行為構成（如同所有行為一樣，皆有終盡）。古人知曉此理（最上等的阿格尼祭），因此不奉獻（普通的）阿格尼祭。

6. 烏克他（Uktha）是梵天，舒什卡布利姆伽羅（Sushkabhrmgara）如是說。讓他冥想它（烏克他）與梨克（Rik）相同，則所有眾生將讚美他為最殊勝者。讓他冥想它與雅柱（Ya^-us）相同，則所有眾生將聚集在他面前¹ ¹ 烏克他乃吠陀讚歌，已在《高弗亞》（Kavva）及其他《沙卡》（Sakhas）中被認同為普拉那（pra;za，呼吸）（《勃里哈德阿蘭亞卡奧義書》V, 13, 1；《愛迪太亞》II, 1, 2）。此處烏克他，即烏克他之普拉那，進一步被認同為梵天。由於烏克他（讚歌）是普拉那，且祭祀以讚歌進行，祭祀亦為烏克他，因此為普拉那，因此為梵天。——註疏者

讓他把它當作與薩曼（Saman）相同的來冥想，所有眾生都會在他面前俯首稱臣，視他為最優越者。讓他把它當作與力量相同的來冥想，讓他把它當作與榮光相同的來冥想，讓他把它當作與光耀相同的來冥想。因為弓在武器中是最強大的、最光榮的、最燦爛的，同樣地，懂得此道的人在所有眾生中也是最強大的、最光榮的、最燦爛的。祭祀僧（Adhvaryu）認為祭壇上的火，即用於祭祀的火，就是他自己。在火中，他（祭祀僧）編織了祭祀的雅諸（Yajus）部分。而在雅諸部分中，誦讚僧（Hotri）編織了梨俱（Rik）部分。而在梨俱部分中，歌讚僧（Udgatri）編織了薩曼部分。他（祭祀僧或生命氣）是三重知識的自我；他實在是它（生命氣）的自我。誰懂得此道，他就是它的自我（成為生命氣）。
動詞 ark、vug 與 sannam 並非按照習用法使用，而是參照 rik、yagus 與 saman 等詞彙而來。

2 注釋者對此有不同的解釋。他認為前一段的目的是表明原始智識（Prawa-vidya）最終能夠產生最終的解脫，而不僅是暫時的回報。他說，祭司（Adhvaryu priest）取所謂的讚美詩（uktha），已被認定為梨俱吠陀、夜柔吠陀和娑摩吠陀讚美詩，都蘊含在口中，作為外在祭壇的祭火，因為該火若無此等讚美詩不能點燃。因此祭司的自我不僅與讚美詩（uktha）相同一，也與祭火相同一，他觀想自己為火、為讚美詩（uktha）、為呼吸（prāṇa）。我讀作「他以此三重知識（trayyai vidyaya）的自我存在；他正是自我。凡知此者，即成此自我」（sa esha sarvasyai trayyai vidyaya atma, esha u evasyatma. Etadatma bhavati ya eva veda）。但若我們讀作 asyatma，則不能如注釋者所言以「此三重讚美詩之自我」（asya uktayas trayya atma）來解釋，而必須將 asya 指向呼吸（prāṇa）、生命（life），此處應與梵天（Brahman）相同一。
II ADHYAYA, 8. 285 7. 其次是全能者（sarva^it）考希達基（Kaushitaki）的三種冥想。全能的考希達基在日出時敬拜太陽，已經穿上犧牲繩，已經取來水，並且三次灑淨水杯，說：「你是解救者，請將我從罪惡中解救出來。」同樣的方式，他在日中時敬拜太陽，說：「你是最高的解救者，請將我從罪惡中高度地解救出來。」同樣的方式，他在日落時敬拜太陽，說：
「汝乃圓滿之解救者，願汝使我圓滿地脫離罪惡。」如此，他便圓滿地消除了他白日與夜晚所犯的一切罪惡。同樣地，知曉此理者亦如是祭祀太陽，並圓滿地消除他白日與夜晚所犯的一切罪惡。
8. 其次，讓他在每月新月之時向西方顯現之月亮禮拜，儀式與（前述關於太陽之方式）相同，或者讓他持兩片青草葉向月亮傳達其言語，言曰：「嗚呼！汝乃不朽喜樂之女主，藉由我心中所居於月亮之溫柔之心，願我對於吾兒女之不幸永不哭泣。」
他（如此崇拜月亮之人）的子嗣在他之前確實不會死亡。對於已經生有兒子的人而言，情形就是如此。
現在是那尚未生有兒子的人。他低聲吟誦三首梨俱吠陀詩句：「增長吧，俄‧蘇摩！願力量降臨於你」(Rv. I, 91, 16；IX, 31, 4)。
